stories.headline: Won Slides Toward Weakest Since 2009 as Global Funds Sell Stocks
stories.summary: South Korea’s won slid toward its weakest level since the global financial crisis, leading a retreat in Asian currencies, as the dollar strengthened and overseas investors sold local equities.

stories.headline: Trump Reports $1.4 Billion in 2025 Crypto Earnings
stories.summary: President Donald Trump reported earning at least $1.4 billion in 2025 from crypto and memecoin-related businesses, according to his latest annual financial disclosure. Bloomberg's Kailey Leinz and Romaine Bostick break it down. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office
stories.summary: The president's crypto income far outpaces his earnings from real estate and Trump-themed items such as watches.

stories.headline: Germany woos Trump with plan to make US weapons in Europe
stories.summary: Talks on ‘joint production concepts’ accelerate ahead of Nato summit

stories.headline: Planes leaving half full in EU border chaos, says industry
stories.summary: Airports and airlines warn delays will worsen over summer unless they are allowed to abandon new checks

stories.headline: Goldman Flags Up Oil Surplus Even as Nations Rebuild Stockpiles
stories.summary: The global oil market is set to swing back into oversupply as the impact of the Iran war fades and traffic through the Strait of Hormuz recovers, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

stories.headline: Iran War Delivers Massive Swings in Chinese Petrochemicals Trade
stories.summary: The war in Iran has rewired China’s petrochemicals trade, allowing plants to ease their glut of the key building blocks for plastics, rubber and textiles by boosting exports.

stories.headline: World’s oceans experience hottest June ever, scientists say more heat ahead
stories.summary: European Union monitors say the first half of 2026 was 'marked by sustained and exceptional ocean warmth'.

stories.headline: Iran says it couldn’t export a ‘single barrel of oil’ during US blockade
stories.summary: Iran was unable to export any oil during the US blockade of its ports, the country's chief negotiator has said.

stories.headline: Who is leading the race for the FIFA World Cup 2026 Golden Boot award?
stories.summary: Argentina's Messi is level on goals with France's Mbappe as World Cup favourites look to power through the knockouts.

stories.headline: Iran leaves World Cup with warm send-off from Mexican fans
stories.summary: Mexican fans gathered in Tijuana to bid farewell to Iran's national football team before the squad departed

stories.headline: Japan’s FX Chief Says Past Yen Intervention Has Shown Impact
stories.summary: Foreign exchange intervention by Japan to support the yen two months ago was successful, and some US authorities voiced support, the nation’s top currency official said, as the yen hovers at a four-decade low.

stories.headline: Traders on Lookout for Next Yen 'Red Line' | The Asia Trade 7/1/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: RBI’s Short Dollar Book Hit Record in May Before Policy Measures
stories.summary: The Reserve Bank of India’s short dollar book rose to a record $106.7 billion in May as the central bank used derivatives to defend the currency.

stories.headline: Aluminum Falls to Lowest Since February as Dollar Strengthens
stories.summary: Aluminum fell to the lowest since mid-February as a strengthening US dollar continued to pressure commodities.

stories.headline: India Prop Traders Brace for RBI Funding Squeeze
stories.summary: Traders expect reduced returns from cash-futures arbitrage, options market making and index arbitrage.

stories.headline: Kotak Reclaims India ECM Lead With Mega Deals on Horizon
stories.summary: Kotak Mahindra Capital Co. reclaimed the top spot in India’s equity capital markets league table for the first half of the year, putting it in position to dominate a year with the country’s largest-ever initial public offerings in the pipeline.

stories.headline: Gojek co-founder, turned Indonesian Education Minister jailed for 10 years
stories.summary: Gojek co-founder and former Education Minister Nadiem Makarim has been sentenced to 10 years in prison.

stories.headline: Algeria to vote in test of post-Hirak political landscape
stories.summary: Algeria holds legislative elections amid debates over reform, turnout and political stability.

stories.headline: North Korea’s Kim hails ‘unshakeable will’ to develop ties with China’s Xi
stories.summary: Kim Jong Un has sent Xi Jinping a congratulatory message to mark the 105th anniversary of the Chinese Communist Party.

stories.headline: US Supreme Court has dealt heavy defeats to Trump, while expanding his power
stories.summary: The birthright ruling brings to an end a Supreme Court term that has delivered the president some key victories.

stories.headline: Trump Says It’s ‘Too Bad’ Supreme Court Upheld Birthright Citizenship
stories.summary: The court’s decision was a major rebuke to President Trump, who falsely asserted that Congress could reverse it through legislation.

stories.headline: ‘No MiGs for Ukraine’: Polish defence minister claims Kyiv backed out of deal with Warsaw
stories.summary: As relations between Ukraine and Poland remain at their worst state in years, the Polish defence minister claimed that Kyiv backed out of the two countries' "MiGs in exchange for drones" deal and threatened to veto Ukraine’s EU accession.

stories.headline: Ukraine in talks with France on SCALP missile licence, defence minister says
stories.summary: Ukraine’s defence minister hailed the talks as an important first step toward expanding the country’s production of critical weapons, amid Kyiv’s push to obtain a US licence for Patriot interceptors.

stories.headline: Thousands of anti-migrant protesters march in South Africa amid heavy police presence
stories.summary: Foreigners have already left South Africa ahead of the deadline set by anti-migrant groups.

stories.headline: Supreme Court upholds principle that almost all born on U.S. soil are American
stories.summary: The ruling striking down President Donald Trump's executive order on birthright citizenship reaffirmed that the 14th Amendment automatically confers citizenship on any child born in the U.S.

stories.headline: China is a clear winner from Trump’s war in Middle East, report concludes
stories.summary: Beijing, whose stockpiles and renewables industry allowed it to withstand energy shock, is now gaining from global solar and EV pushChina has emerged as the sole winner in Asia from the strait of Hormuz crisis, according to a report published on Tuesday.The report by the geopolitical consulting firm Asia Group concluded that China had weathered the storm of the global commodities crisis resulting from the closure of the Middle Eastern waterway, a

stories.headline: US Supreme Court upholds bans on transgender athletes in female school and college sports
stories.summary: President Trump called the ruling a "big win", while an LGBT group said it was "heartbreaking".

stories.headline: Supreme Court's birthright ruling is major blow to Trump

stories.headline: Why are South Korean fans so angry at their football coach?

stories.headline: Dollar Gains Ahead of Warsh Speech, Stocks Waver: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: The dollar strengthened against all its Group-of-10 peers as traders awaited key speeches from a swath of central bankers, including Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh, just as bets increased for an interest-rate hike.

stories.headline: Alcoa Bets on Aluminum Boom With $5.6 Billion South32 Deal
stories.summary: Alcoa Corp. has agreed to buy South32 Ltd.’s bauxite, alumina and aluminum assets in a deal worth as much as $5.6 billion, cementing its position as a top producer as long-term demand strengthens and the Iran war exposes supply concerns.

stories.headline: 4 Warning Signs for Graham Platner. And 4 for Susan Collins Too.
stories.summary: The Maine race, which both parties see as key to winning control of the Senate, is extremely close, a Times/Portland Press Herald/Siena poll found. Both candidates have reasons for concern.

stories.headline: Police units deployed across South Africa before anti-immigration marches

stories.headline: Six feared dead after ‘bizarre’ sinking of charter boat off Canadian coast

stories.headline: Monaco bombing was ‘attempted assassination’, not terror attack, say prosecutors – as it happened

stories.headline: Two gas fields off Cyprus could start production by 2033
stories.summary: Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides lauded the signing of the "declaration of marketability" as a "major step towards establishing the Eastern Mediterranean as a credible alternative energy corridor for Europe".

stories.headline: A plane crashed into a tower in Beijing but China is not saying what happened

stories.headline: Afghan Taliban launch strikes on border with Pakistan as tensions escalate
stories.summary: Pakistan's military says it shot down four rudimentary drones and will respond to any further provocation.

stories.headline: Almost 60,000 far-right extremists in Germany, intelligence agency says

stories.headline: What to know about the looming  deadline on North American free trade
stories.summary: The US, Canada and Mexico were to reach an agreement on whether to extend their free trade pact by 1 July. All signs point to them blowing past that deadline.

stories.headline: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
stories.summary: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

stories.headline: Supreme Court, for now, blocks Trump from firing Fed board member Lisa Cook
stories.summary: The decision is a setback for Trump, who alleged that Cook committed mortgage fraud. She is the first Black woman to serve on the Fed board.

stories.headline: Supreme Court lets stand $5 million civil verdict against Trump in Carroll case
stories.summary: A 2023 trial found President Donald Trump liable for allegedly sexually assaulting journalist E. Jean Carroll. The decision Monday leaves in place a ruling affirming the judgment.

stories.headline: South Korea unveils $880bn chip and AI investment plan
stories.summary: It comes as regional rivals like Taiwan, China and Japan are investing heavily in chip factories and other technology.

stories.headline: EU sets up three months of talks with China over €360bn trade deficit
stories.summary: Two sides agree to try to make bilateral relationship ‘more balanced’ after weeks of threatsThe EU and China have agreed to enter three months of talks to try to avoid a trade war over the bloc’s €360bn (£310bn) annual import/export imbalance.In their first joint statement in seven years, the two sides agreed in Brussels to open a formal trade consultation after weeks of threats and recriminations from China if the EU imposed any measures to stop

stories.headline: Pakistani strikes kill at least 28 civilians in Afghanistan, UN says
stories.summary: Pakistan claims it was targeting militants near its border, but the Taliban government calls it an "atrocity".

stories.headline: South Korea football coach quits as president calls for probe into World Cup loss
stories.summary: South Korea was eliminated after missing out on a spot among the eight best third-placed teams.

stories.headline: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline

stories.headline: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany

stories.summary: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.

stories.headline: Would farmers and fishermen back Brexit today?
stories.summary: Polls suggested the majority of farmers and fishers in England voted to leave the EU 10 years ago.
